data.title: Fed loses access to separate employment data from third-party provider
data.description: Nick Timiraos, a prominent Fed spokesperson, wrote that Federal Reserve officials recently lost access to third-party employment data. Since 2018, payroll processor ADP has provided the Fed with a dataset containing anonymous employment and income information, covering 20% ​​of the US private sector workforce. The Fed typically receives this data with a delay of approximately one week, making it a timely and comprehensive measure of labor market conditions. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that ADP stopped providing the Fed with this data after Fed Governor Waller's speech in late August, drawing public attention to the Fed's long-standing use of ADP employment data. The specific reasons for this change are unclear. Waller cited the ADP data in a footnote accompanying his speech, further emphasizing his concerns about a slowing labor market. The footnote noted that preliminary estimates showed a continued deterioration in hiring over the summer, and that the data covered a period beyond the coverage of the latest government data. (Jinshi)

data.title: a16z: Stablecoin annual transaction volume reaches $46 trillion, 20 times that of PayPal
data.description: The a16z report, "State of Crypto 2025," notes that the crypto market continues to expand, exhibiting a "price-developer-user" feedback loop. Crypto adoption is global, but the fastest-growing on-chain activity is in developing countries. Developers are most active in multi-chain ecosystems such as Ethereum (including L2), Solana, and Bitcoin. Furthermore, stablecoins have become a widely discussed topic, with annual transaction volume reaching $46 trillion, 20 times that of PayPal and 3 times that of Visa.

data.title: ENA plummeted 10% in a short period of time, and the address associated with Andrew Kang on Hyperliquid was liquidated.
data.description: According to MLM monitoring, an hour ago, ENA plummeted 10% in just 2 minutes, once again liquidating the address associated with Andrew Kang on Hyperliquid. The total liquidation amount of this address on ENA was US$47.38 million, of which approximately US$8.5 million in replenishment funds were also liquidated.

data.title: Specific NFT holders in the MegaETH presale can receive higher amounts

data.title: Trust Wallet Launches Perpetual Contract Trading, Powered by Aster DEX
data.description: Trust Wallet announced on the X platform that it has launched perpetual swaps (Perps) trading within its app. This feature supports over 100 markets with up to 100x leverage, powered by Aster DEX.

data.title: [LIVE] Trump Pardons CZ – Tracking the Impact on BNB and Aster Prices
data.description:
President Trump has issued a pardon for Binance co-founder Changpeng “CZ” Zhao, who had pled guilty in 2024 and has served four months, per the Wall Street Journal. BNB has reached $1,150 before easing to $1,128, up roughly 4. 5% in recent hours.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/live-trump-pardons-cz-tracking-the-impact-on-bnb-and-aster-prices/

data.title: Pundit Drops Bombshell Exposé On Kadena Team After Closure Announcement Saw KDA Price Crash Over 60%
data.description:
This week, the cryptocurrency community was rocked after Kadena’s sudden shutdown announcement sent the KDA price crashing by over 60% in a few hours. The massive price collapse triggered an enormous sell-off as investors scrambled to understand the abrupt closure of the once-promising blockchain project. Soon after, a shocking exposé from analysts revealed that the problems ran far deeper than market conditions, hinting at serious internal misconduct and mismanagement. Kadena Scandal Exposed After KDA Price Crash A day after the KDA price crash on Tuesday, crypto analyst Lovrin revealed on X social media that several Kadena employees were allegedly caught shorting the token with leverage just before shutdown announcements, securing tens of millions of dollars in profits
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/pundit-drops-crypto-bombshell/
